arts.ttl: ‘He is fine’ – Hull City man ‘ready’ to face Swansea City as Ruben Selles shares update
arts.sum: Hull City boss Ruben Selles has said John Egan is ‘fine’ to face Swansea City on Friday. Hull… The post ‘He is fine’ – Hull City man ‘ready’ to face Swansea City as Ruben Selles shares update appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: 'Nobody ever downs tools': Reading boss on 'organised and strong' Lincoln City threat
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt is wary of the threat posed by Lincoln City, even if the Imps have little left to play for. The Royals sit sixth in League One and have four matches remaining to confirm a play-off position, including a second match in quick succession at Mansfield Town on Easter Monday. Bristol Rovers are battling relegation, as were Northampton Town last week, while Barnsley battle Blackpool for a top 10 finish. Lincoln drew with soon-to-be-relegated Shrewsbury Town on Saturday and are 12th in the third tier, with little but pride and contracts to play for.

Reading might have slipped up at struggling Northampton last time out, but results went their way, meaning they ended up extending the gap to seventh. But both Leyton Orient and Bolton are still with...

arts.ttl: Reading boss leaning on Brendan Rodgers experience to help Harlee Dean coaching plan
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt is using his experiences as a player under Brendan Rodgers to help experienced defender Harlee Dean 'dip his toe' into the coaching world. The former Birmingham City and Brentford favourite is awaiting an appointment with a specialist for a hip injury and is unlikely to feature again for the rest of the season. Now 33 and out of contract in the summer, the centre-back has consistently spoken of his ambitions to move into the world of coaching, highlight former boss Ruben Selles as an individual of some inspiration.

arts.ttl: Reading boss issues full Lincoln City injury update- including star goalkeeper
arts.sum: Reading goalkeeper Joel Pereira should be 'okay' to continue starting between the sticks as his side hunt down the play-offs despite picking up an injury last week. The 29-year-old limped off during the warm-up before Saturday's 0-0 draw with Northampton Town but battled through to play the entire match. Manager Noel Hunt confirmed the keeper and Lewis Wing, who landed awkwardly late in the game, would be assessed before Good Friday's meeting with Lincoln City. Giving the Portuguese shot stopper extra time to recover, Hunt has confirmed they are both eligible for selection.

arts.ttl: Reading legend pips Noel Hunt to League One manager prize ahead of run-in
arts.sum: Reading legend Phil Parkinson pipped current manager Noel Hunt to the League One Manager of the Month award, despite the Royals edging Wrexham in March. Hunt was nominated after meandering the month unbeaten, a run of 11 matches which ended in Blackpool in April. Picking up 12 points from six matches in the month, including wins over Wrexham and Peterborough United, the Royals put themselves firmly in the hunt for promotion. Darren Ferguson of Peterborough United and Nathan Jones of Charlton Athletic were also nominated, however it was Parkinson of Wrexham who earned the prize.
